raw.objective: “to describe possible effect of frequently co-administered medications (dopamine, dobutamine, norepinephrine, furosemide, phenytoin, midazolanm, sufentanil, and tramadol) on the PB pharmacokinetics in full-term newborns with HIE”
raw.covariates: Tested: comedication with dopamine, dobutamine, norepinephrine, furosemide, phenytoin, midazolanm, sufentanil, or tramadol on CL Included: WT and comedication (dopamine) on CL; WT on Vd
raw.observations: Only dopamine had a statistically significant effect on PHB clearance. The study only included 37 newborns, which may be too small a sample size to adequately determine statistically significant differences.

extraction_target.title: Effect of co-medication on the pharmacokinetic parameters of phenobarbital in asphyxiated newborns.
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to describe possible effect of frequently co-administered medications (dopamine, dobutamine, norepinephrine, furosemide, phenytoin, midazolanm, sufentanil, and tramadol) on the PB pharmacokinetics in full-term newborns with HIE”

covariates.raw: Tested: comedication with dopamine, dobutamine, norepinephrine, furosemide, phenytoin, midazolanm, sufentanil, or tramadol on CL Included: WT and comedication (dopamine) on CL; WT on Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
